phase_name: Ollama backend reliability: diagnostics, empty-transcript guard, and LLM input logging
goal: Address two observed failure modes when using the Ollama backend: (1) the LLM returns a generic 'I'm sorry—I'm having trouble understanding' response; (2) the LLM produces nothing, leaving the clipboard empty or containing only whitespace. Root causes: an empty or near-empty transcript is passed to the LLM without any guard, and ollama can silently return an empty streaming response with exit 0. Additionally, add a per-call JSON log of LLM inputs and outputs to ~/.config/yaptap/logs/ to aid post-hoc debugging.

tasks.title: Log a WARNING in `stream_response()` when transcript is empty or whitespace-only
tasks.description:
In `src/core/llm.py`, inside `stream_response()`, add an empty-transcript warning immediately after the prompt file is loaded and `messages` is built — before dispatching to any provider.

Add:
```python
if not transcript.strip():
    logger.warning(
        "Empty transcript passed to LLM; model response may be unhelpful",
        extra={"provider": provider, "model": model_name},
    )
```

This surfaces the root cause of the 'I'm sorry' response in stderr/logs when it happens. Follow python-logging.mdc: constant message string, runtime values in `extra`.

This is a diagnostic only — per the spec (specs/llm.md § Error Cases), an empty transcript is still forwarded to the provider.
checks: `grep 'Empty transcript passed to LLM' src/core/llm.py` returns a match.

tasks.title: Raise `RuntimeError` in `stream_response()` when the LLM yields zero tokens
tasks.description:
In `src/core/llm.py`, modify both the `ollama` and `compactifai` branches of `stream_response()` to detect and reject an empty streaming response.

For each branch, add a `yielded_any` flag:
```python
yielded_any = False
for chunk in response:
    token = ...  # existing extraction
    if token:
        yielded_any = True
    yield token
if not yielded_any:
    raise RuntimeError(
        "LLM returned empty response — transcript may be empty or model unresponsive"
    )
```

Placing the raise **after** the for loop (not inside it) preserves the generator contract: the exception is only raised when the caller has exhausted the iterator.

This converts the silent 'nothing on clipboard' failure into an explicit `RuntimeError` that propagates through `main()` to stderr and exit 1, which Rust surfaces as a pipeline error alert.

Update the docstring Raises section to document this new RuntimeError.
checks: `grep 'LLM returned empty response' src/core/llm.py` returns a match.
Both the ollama and compactifai branches contain `yielded_any` logic (`grep 'yielded_any' src/core/llm.py` returns ≥2 matches).

tasks.title: Fix ollama chunk token access to use attribute access (`chunk.message.content`)
tasks.description:
In `src/core/llm.py`, the ollama branch currently uses dict-style access:
```python
yield chunk["message"]["content"]
```

The `ollama` Python library v0.3+ returns `ChatResponse` pydantic objects that use attribute access, not `__getitem__`. Dict-style access raises `TypeError` on newer versions, which is caught by the `except Exception` and re-raised as `RuntimeError`, surfacing as an error dialog.

Change to attribute access:
```python
yield chunk.message.content
```

Update the mock in `src/core/llm_test.py` `test_stream_response_yields_tokens_on_valid_input` to use objects with attribute access instead of raw dicts:
```python
chunk1 = MagicMock()
chunk1.message.content = "Hello"
chunk2 = MagicMock()
chunk2.message.content = " world"
mock_response = [chunk1, chunk2]
```

This matches the pattern already used in the CompactifAI test (`test_stream_response_compactifai_yields_tokens`).
checks: `grep 'chunk.message.content' src/core/llm.py` returns a match in the ollama branch.
`grep 'chunk\[' src/core/llm.py` returns no match (dict-style access removed).
python Black formatting passes on `src/core/llm.py` and `src/core/llm_test.py`.

tasks.title: Read stdout and stderr concurrently in `run_llm_collect()` to prevent pipe deadlock
Add empty-transcript guard in `run_pipeline_inner()` in `src/app.rs`
checks: `grep 'No speech detected' src/app.rs` returns a match.
Guard is inside the `if !config.selected_prompt.is_empty()` block, not at the top of `run_pipeline_inner`.

tasks.title: Add `--log-dir` optional argument to `llm.py`
tasks.description:
In `src/core/llm.py`, add an optional `--log-dir` argument to the `argparse` block in `main()`:

```python
parser.add_argument(
    "--log-dir",
    default=None,
    help="Directory for LLM call log files (created if absent). If omitted, no log is written.",
)
```

Pass `args.log_dir` to the log-writing function added in P14-T007.

Update the module-level docstring to mention the `--log-dir` flag.
checks: `grep '\-\-log-dir' src/core/llm.py` returns a match in the argparse block.

tasks.title: Implement `_write_llm_log()` in `llm.py` and call from `main()`
tasks.description:
In `src/core/llm.py`, add a private helper function:

```python
def _write_llm_log(
    log_dir: str,
    provider: str,
    model: str,
    prompt_path: str,
    system_prompt: str,
    transcript: str,
    response: str,
    error: str | None,
) -> None:
    """Append a JSON call record to <log_dir>/llm_calls.jsonl."""
    import json
    from datetime import datetime, timezone
    from pathlib import Path

    log_path = Path(log_dir)
    try:
        log_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        record = {
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
            "provider": provider,
            "model": model,
            "prompt_path": prompt_path,
            "system_prompt": system_prompt,
            "transcript": transcript,
            "response": response,
            "error": error,
        }
        with (log_path / "llm_calls.jsonl").open("a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
            fh.write(json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
    except Exception as exc:  # log errors must never crash the pipeline
        logger.warning("Failed to write LLM log", extra={"log_dir": log_dir, "error": str(exc)})
```

In `main()`, collect the full response into a string while streaming, then call `_write_llm_log()` at the end:

```python
response_parts: list[str] = []
try:
    for token in stream_response(transcript, args.prompt_file, args.model, args.provider):
        print(token, end="", flush=True)
        response_parts.append(token)
except (ValueError, RuntimeError) as err:
    if args.log_dir:
        _write_llm_log(
            args.log_dir, args.provider, args.model, args.prompt_file,
            "",  # system_prompt not extracted here; use empty string
            transcript, "", str(err),
        )
    print(f"error: {err}", file=sys.stderr)
    sys.exit(1)

if args.log_dir:
    _write_llm_log(
        args.log_dir, args.provider, args.model, args.prompt_file,
        "",  # system prompt already validated; omit to avoid re-reading file
        transcript, "".join(response_parts), None,
    )
print()  # final newline
```

Note: the `system_prompt` field is left empty in `main()` to avoid re-reading the TOML file after `stream_response()` has already consumed it. A future task could pass it through if needed.

The log format is JSON Lines (one record per line in `llm_calls.jsonl`). This file appends across sessions and is easy to inspect with `tail`, `jq`, etc.

Errors from `_write_llm_log()` are caught internally and logged as WARNING — they must never propagate and interrupt the pipeline.
checks: `grep '_write_llm_log' src/core/llm.py` returns matches for the definition and both call sites (success and error paths).

Calling `python3 src/core/llm.py --prompt-file <valid.toml> --log-dir /tmp/yaptap_log_test` with transcript on stdin produces a file at `/tmp/yaptap_log_test/llm_calls.jsonl` containing a valid JSON object per line.
Errors from `_write_llm_log()` are caught — validated by passing a non-writable log_dir and confirming the main flow still exits 0.

tasks.title: Add `log_dir: Option<&Path>` parameter to `run_llm_collect()` in `src/llm.rs`
tasks.description:
Extend `run_llm_collect()` in `src/llm.rs` to accept an optional log directory path and forward it as `--log-dir` to `llm.py`.

New signature (add parameter after `active_child`):
```rust
pub fn run_llm_collect(
    transcript: &str,
    prompt_path: &Path,
    model: &str,
    provider: &str,
    api_key: Option<&str>,
    active_child: &Arc<Mutex<Option<Child>>>,
    log_dir: Option<&Path>,
) -> Result<String>
```

In the command builder block, after the existing `--provider` args, add:
```rust
if let Some(dir) = log_dir {
    cmd.args(["--log-dir", &dir.to_string_lossy()]);
}
```

Update the `/// # Contract` doc comment to document the new `log_dir` parameter.

All callers of `run_llm_collect` must be updated to pass the new argument (see P14-T009 for `app.rs`).
checks: `grep 'log_dir' src/llm.rs` returns matches in the signature, command builder block, and doc comment.

tasks.title: Resolve `~/.config/yaptap/logs/` and pass to `run_llm_collect()` in `src/app.rs`
tasks.description:
In `src/app.rs`, inside `run_pipeline_inner()`, resolve the log directory and pass it to `run_llm_collect()`.

Before the `run_llm_collect()` call, add:
```rust
let log_dir = dirs::home_dir().map(|h| h.join(".config/yaptap/logs"));
```

Update the `run_llm_collect()` call to pass `log_dir.as_deref()`:
```rust
crate::llm::run_llm_collect(
    &transcript,
    &prompt_path,
    &config.llm_model,
    &config.llm_provider,
    api_key.as_deref(),
    &shared.child,
    log_dir.as_deref(),
)
```

`dirs::home_dir()` is already a dependency (used elsewhere in `app.rs`). No new crate required.
checks: Running the app with a valid prompt and recording produces a file at `~/.config/yaptap/logs/llm_calls.jsonl` containing a JSON record with non-empty `transcript` and `response` fields.

tasks.title: Pass `--log-dir ~/.config/yaptap/logs` to inline `llm.py` spawn in `src/main.rs`
checks: Running `yaptap --prompt <name>` (CLI mode) produces a record in `~/.config/yaptap/logs/llm_calls.jsonl`.
`grep 'log.dir' src/main.rs` returns a match in the inline spawn block.

tasks.title: Add test for empty-transcript WARNING log in `llm_test.py`
tasks.description:
Add `test_stream_response_warns_on_empty_transcript()` to `src/core/llm_test.py`.

- Create a valid temporary TOML prompt file.
- Patch `ollama.chat` to return an iterator with one non-empty chunk (so the test does not raise on zero tokens — that is tested separately in P14-T012).
- Capture log records using `self.assertLogs('src.core.llm', level='WARNING')` context manager.
- Call `list(stream_response("", toml_path))` (empty transcript).
- Assert at least one WARNING record contains `'Empty transcript'`.
- Include a What/Why docstring following the pattern in `llm_test.py`.

tasks.title: Add test for zero-token `RuntimeError` in `llm_test.py`
tasks.description:
Add `test_stream_response_raises_on_empty_ollama_response()` to `src/core/llm_test.py`.

- Create a valid temporary TOML prompt file.
- Patch `ollama.chat` to return an empty iterator (`iter([])`).
- Call `list(stream_response("some transcript", toml_path))`.
- Assert `RuntimeError` is raised with a message containing `'empty response'`.
- Include a What/Why docstring.

Also add `test_stream_response_raises_on_empty_compactifai_response()`:
- Set `MULTIVERSE_IAM_API_KEY` in env.
- Mock `openai.OpenAI` client so `chat.completions.create()` returns an empty iterator.
- Assert `RuntimeError` with `'empty response'`.
- Clean up env after test.

tasks.title: Add test for log file creation in `llm_test.py`
tasks.description:
Add `test_write_llm_log_creates_jsonl_with_expected_fields()` to `src/core/llm_test.py`.

- Import `_write_llm_log` from `src.core.llm` (add to `__all__` or import directly).
- Use `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` as the log directory.
- Call `_write_llm_log(tmpdir, 'ollama', 'llama3', '/tmp/p.toml', 'Be helpful', 'hello', 'world', None)`.
- Assert that `<tmpdir>/llm_calls.jsonl` exists.
- Parse the first line as JSON.
- Assert the record contains keys: `timestamp`, `provider`, `model`, `prompt_path`, `system_prompt`, `transcript`, `response`, `error`.
- Assert `record['provider'] == 'ollama'` and `record['transcript'] == 'hello'`.
- Call `_write_llm_log()` a second time and assert the file now contains exactly two lines (JSONL append).
- Include a What/Why docstring.
checks: `python -m unittest src.core.llm_test` runs all tests and passes.
